content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Morning, Germany.

content.speaker: jeremy.campeau@llsa.com
content.text: Good morning. How are you?

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Not bad. Not bad yourself. While I have you, I did send you that one ticket that we spoke about yesterday.

content.text: Six?

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
It's just... It's really more just proposing the scheme. I'm more looking for research.
Then, maybe just a confluence page on what you think the schema might look like, and then we can have a conversation about it.

content.text:
Okay, yeah, I was looking through it yesterday, writing down some questions. I'm actually trying to write out a scheme, and now... So I'll put up a confluence page under that area and start writing stuff.
Then throw my questions too.

content.text: Yes. Yeah. So Antonio is probably the best person to start with. He'll coordinate with Sam. Alright's... Yeah, I think my worry there is don't run independently on it, just do your investigation, get his thoughts. His work is in process or in flow, so there may be changes coming out of that.

content.text: Okay, I'll ping in with my questions then. Is that what you're saying? Okay.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
I just don't want to spend too much time cycling.
All right? Jiffco, Francis, I'd love to have him here. Actually, maybe you have to go. I know it's early, but have you ever had a chance to connect with Francis, by any chance? Or see his last comments?

content.speaker: Speaker 3
content.text: No, I have not connected to Francis yet.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: No, okay.

content.speaker: Speaker 3
content.text: Let's see about his comments.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
All right, well let me just maybe just rephrase the conversation. I saw the conversation generally around the blue green. I know that you and me how are me how did some initial review for you just wanted to the goal that we had or what was set to these yesterday was we're going to target trying to get some of these flagged as green these being just the stuff on the ingestion side inside of the MIRR and document.
I'll share my screen. So I don't want to go back to space and just not have a clear reason why these are not green. So I just wanted to... If you and Francis can connect before standup...
I know standup is in half an hour, so maybe we can just step away now and give you guys a chance to connect. That's more important to me than getting a clear status on this before 11:00. It's really what I need to get for Stacey to three.

content.speaker: Speaker 3
content.text:
I can give you the status right now. It's straightforward. So I'm merging currently my changes. The merch queue estimates about eight minutes from now to merge, so probably ten more minutes to actually deploy and merge, then validate and connect to Francis to see if all the domains and everything is there. He said there are...
But just to check every piece after the merch. If anything is broken, obviously you have to work to fix it, but hopefully it's not. I did some patches. So it seems... Shouldn't be bog 31.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
Yeah. So great. Let's say that sounds like 30 minutes ish for that effort since it's already in the merge queue and that will effectively prove out or you'll validate that we have a blue-green instance for all of these resources within... We have a blue-green instance for all these instances. My question would be, does that include Islam?
Because, Antonio, we're reworking this quite a bit, so this may be moot, that point may be moot.

content.speaker: Speaker 3
content.text: Yes.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
Okay. So then the answer is you're still work in progress on that. We're focused solely around this, okay?
All right, Justo, that's good news. Please, as soon as you have that, just let me know or feel free to hit it in status.

content.speaker: Speaker 3
content.text: Sounds good.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Okay. You had a couple of things that are not... That are more status. But if you have anything pertaining to what Jiffco just mentioned, if you had a chance to review, welcome your thoughts as well.

content.text:
I already reviewed one PR, so it was the one about blue-green. I approved it, so I think it's good to go. Other than that, I don't have any updates on this thing. So I'm ready to start working on the ticket that Sam suggested I should work on.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Yeah, I still... Okay, the action at Sam, remind me what that was again, please.

content.text:
Okay, I'll find it. Don't worry about it. Okay, all good, let's keep going. Antonio, anything? Well, actually before Antonio... Jeremy, now that we're pivoting away from here, is this merged into Tours already?
It's tested. It's in quote unquote production on that side and just needs to add additional validation.

content.text: No, I still got it reviewed. I was going to be with Lance today just to go over it. He's aware of how it works. Just in case something happens or ever makes sure that it's looking good from what he knows about CSTAR and recurly and stuff.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: So it's there's still a bit of a review there. No worries.

content.speaker: jeremy.campeau@llsa.com
content.text: Correct?

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:

But like the final point, like being able to do a diff against them. Do you want a ticket for actually doing that investigation? Do you have a sense of what that could be already?

content.speaker: jeremy.campeau@llsa.com
content.text:
I think there's at least going to need to be something where whatever we're doing for testing, we need to look at CTAR and FSA beyond that. I'm not really sure. I can't remember if I mentioned to you, but I know Devon helped with the QA on the Shopify project, so he might actually already have something he used there to validate stuff.
So maybe there's something that he has we could use as a starting point for how we can make sure that those things coming from an integration look good.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
Okay, yeah, all right, no problem. I owe Devon a follow-up anyway, and me... What I think I remember from yesterday was around just the blue-green support. He wanted you to pivot and help validate the blue-green environment.
So you already did that. So I think stay course, focus on what you have for the tracking and the chat feature.

content.speaker: Michal Kawka
content.text: Truth thing makes sense.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Okay, cool. Antonio, larger conversation there. So, anything you want to share on this? Or do you think just still work in progress on the...?

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Yeah, let's do that later, please. And then?

content.speaker: Speaker 5

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Okay, and then... Lens to you.

content.speaker: Speaker 5

content.speaker: Speaker 5
content.text: It is a variable. Yeah.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Okay, cool, all right, perfect. So... Lance, you're going to have a...

So you mentioned I had a question, you mentioned blue-green for this keys stack that's sent into event grid. I guess my only question there would be we don't have the concept of blue-green on Azure event grid.
So if something is on an inactive leg, for instance, it's still going to be sending to the live event grid.

content.text: That is a really good point and something that we should address in architecture, which is a fair point.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:

content.text: So one more question, just to be clear. You do want me to set up a standalone deployment for this? It wouldn't tie into any of our other workflows, but we would want a mechanism for just deploying this on its own and then perhaps giving Antonio the... So he can work with it.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
Yes, from the last two conversations, we had the idea we had agreed and settled on the idea that these are mini stacks. We had an architecture meeting yesterday and we cemented that decision.
I think you all of... Concerns were raised and like his ask is "Let's just bring that up in conversation as instances of the need to create stacks come up." But the general direction is, yes, this can be a mini stack by itself.

content.speaker: lance.fallon@llsa.com

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson

content.text:
That's great.
Awesome.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Okay, sweet.

content.speaker: Speaker 5
content.text: Okay.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text:
All right, that's it. Thank you, guys, so much. CO, as soon as you have something on this, actually, that's not true, just let me know by status because that'll give me enough time to create a talk track for leadership.
Right. Guys, thank you so much.

content.text: Thank you.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: See?

content.speaker: jeremy.campeau@llsa.com
content.text: Thanks.

content.speaker: Wesley Donaldson
content.text: Us.

content.speaker: jeremy.campeau@llsa.com
content.text: Have a good one.
